description_long: This object is a pair of pliers with a robust, angular design, featuring two elongated, straight handles that converge at a central pivot. The jaws are short and open in a wide, U-shaped arc, suitable for gripping or manipulating objects. The handles are uniform in thickness and appear to be made from a single solid material, lacking any visible ergonomic padding or insulation. The surface is matte and dark gray, suggesting a metal construction, likely steel, with no visible decorative elements or branding. The pivot point is not visibly detailed, indicating a simple fixed joint. Overall, the tool is utilitarian and minimalistic, designed for basic gripping and pulling tasks in urban maintenance or repair settings.
description: A dark gray, angular pair of pliers with straight handles and a wide, U-shaped jaw, constructed from solid metal. The design is minimalistic and utilitarian, lacking ergonomic or decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight handles and the open, U-shaped jaws.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the pliers, with both handles and jaws aligned in a narrow plane.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the straight handles and the back of the jaws.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin, linear profile of the tool.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, angular metal handles with no visible insulation or ergonomic shaping.
attribute_jaw_design: Wide, U-shaped jaws with flat gripping surfaces, lacking serrations or wire-cutting features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Simple fixed pivot joint, not adjustable.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth, untextured metal surface on the handles.
